title: Solving Quantitative Reasoning Problems with Language Models
hook: A language model trained on scientific text solves high school and college math problems.
promise: By the end, you will see how Minerva lets language models tackle challenging math and science questions.
prerequisite_note: You only need basic intuition for how language models read and generate text step by step.

glossary.plain: A neural network that predicts the next word in a sequence, trained on lots of text.
Solving problems that require mathematical calculation, logic, or symbolic manipulation.
A large collection of scientific or mathematical documents used for training.
A method where the model is prompted to show step-by-step reasoning, not just an answer.
Showing the model a handful of example questions and answers to guide its behavior.
Problems or data that are different from what the model saw during training.

cards.title: How Language Models Learn Math
body: Discover how Minerva trains language models to solve real math and science problems, not just recite text.

cards.title: Can a text model do math?
body: Imagine a computer reading thousands of math papers and textbooks, then trying to solve advanced math problems itself.
Minerva is a project where researchers trained a language model on scientific text, pushing it to answer real quantitative questions, not just simple calculations.

cards.title: From reading to reasoning
body: First, the model is pre-trained on vast general text, then fine-tuned on technical, math-heavy documents.
Given a math question, the model is prompted to reason step by step, producing detailed solutions instead of just the final answer.
cards.caption: Minerva bridges language and math by learning from scientific writing.

cards.title: How do models handle math questions?
body: Compare a standard language model with Minerva on a challenging math problem.
cards.offNote: Often gives a plausible answer but fails multi-step reasoning or math notation.
cards.onNote: Produces step-by-step solutions, handles symbols, and arrives at correct answers more often.

cards.title: Minerva’s central trick
body: Instead of just feeding the model more data, Minerva carefully selects technical math and science text to fine-tune the model.
This targeted training teaches the model not only to recognize mathematical language but also to perform multi-step quantitative reasoning.

cards.title: How much technical data?
body: See how the amount of technical training data affects Minerva's math problem performance.
cards.low: Model struggles with symbols and multi-step reasoning.
cards.mid: Model gets some simple math right but misses harder steps.
cards.high: Model solves complex quantitative problems with detailed reasoning.

cards.title: Pick Minerva’s ingredients
body: Which components are crucial for getting a language model to solve real math problems?

choices.label: Large model size
choices.note: Gives more capacity for complex reasoning.

choices.label: Technical math corpus
choices.note: Supplies examples of scientific language and notation.

choices.label: Few-shot examples
choices.note: Guides the model with worked sample problems.

choices.label: Fine-tuning
choices.note: Adapts the model to new, specialized data.

choices.label: Evaluation on real exams
choices.note: Tests if the model can truly solve practical problems.

cards.title: Minerva’s leap in math reasoning
body: Minerva achieved benchmark-leading results on challenging math and science benchmarks, including high school and college-level exams.
The model’s performance improved dramatically with technical training, showing it could generalize to new quantitative questions.
cards.caption: Minerva’s targeted training let language models outperform past approaches on real math exams.

cards.title: Why Minerva matters
body: Minerva proved that language models can cross into quantitative reasoning, not just language understanding, if trained with the right data.
This work inspired new research on using LMs as scientific assistants, raising hopes for AI that can solve real technical problems.

cards.title: How Minerva makes LMs do math

recap.label: Pretrain
recap.note: Start with a large language model trained on general text.

recap.label: Fine-tune
recap.note: Adapt the model with scientific and math-heavy documents.

recap.label: Prompt
recap.note: Guide the model to show step-by-step reasoning for each question.

recap.label: Evaluate
recap.note: Test on real math and science exams to measure progress.

cards.title: Solving Quantitative Reasoning Problems with Language Models
body: Read the original Minerva paper to see exactly how language models learned to solve math.
